headline: State assemblymen's declarations reveal 47% have business conflicts
context: A cross-state analysis of voluntary asset declarations found that 47% of state assemblymen hold active business interests in sectors regulated by their state governments.

cards.big: 47% of state assemblymen have business interests in sectors they help regulate
cards.sub: Nearly half of state lawmakers face potential conflicts of interest

cards.big: State constitutions contain no conflict of interest provisions for elected members
cards.sub: Assemblymen can debate and vote on legislation directly affecting their businesses

cards.big: Construction, property, and agriculture are the top 3 conflict sectors identified
cards.sub: State assemblies control land approval, building permits, and agricultural licences

cards.big: Only Selangor and Penang require assemblymen to declare interests before debates
cards.sub: 11 states have no declaration requirement — conflicts remain completely hidden

cards.big: When lawmakers regulate their own businesses, legislation becomes personal financial planning
Should state assemblymen be required to recuse from votes affecting their business interests?
